topic: MCP memory mutation, save, and embedding reconcile write-path review

executor.selfInvocationGuard: Codex runtime executed lineage directly; cli-codex self-invocation is forbidden by its skill contract.

focus: correctness: mutation hooks and reconcile mutation predicates

findingDetails.title: memory_update leaves entity-density routing cache stale after title or trigger phrase changes
findingDetails.evidence: memory_update accepts title/trigger phrase changes, update_memory writes those fields and only clears the general search cache; entity-density's cached token set is built from the same fields and is invalidated only by its dedicated API.
findingDetails.recommendation: Invalidate entity-density after successful update_memory changes to title or trigger_phrases, preferably through the shared post-mutation hook result contract.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed memory_update handler, vector-index mutation helper, mutation hooks, entity-density cache, and entity-density commit-hook tests.

findingDetails.title: memory_embedding_reconcile dry-run under-reports success-coverage repairs for rows missing only the active dimension vector
findingDetails.evidence: computeSuccessCoverage counts only missing vec_memories_rowids, while apply repair resets success rows missing either vec_memories_rowids or the active vec_<dim> row.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make dry-run success coverage use the same OR predicate as apply repair, then add a regression for rowid-present/dim-missing success rows.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared computeSuccessCoverage, plannedMutations, apply repair SQL, and vector-coverage hygiene tests.

claimAdjudications.claim: memory_update can leave entity-density stale after title or trigger phrase changes.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: Checked delete helpers and bulk-delete/save invalidation call sites; delete paths already invalidate through delete_memory_from_database, but update_memory does not.
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: The cache TTL eventually self-heals after 60 seconds, but the documented mutation freshness contract requires immediate post-mutation invalidation for routing correctness.
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if memory_update no longer changes title/trigger_phrases or runPostMutationHooks is updated to clear entity-density before release.

claimAdjudications.claim: dry-run success-coverage repair counts can be lower than apply mutations for dim-only missing success rows.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: Checked retry-eligible missing-vector buckets; those already use the OR predicate. The mismatch is isolated to success coverage.
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: The rowids table may have been treated as the authoritative presence marker, but the apply path explicitly treats either missing rowid or missing dimension row as repairable.
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if success coverage intentionally ignores dim-only gaps and apply is changed to match that narrower predicate.

focus: security: validation, shard authority, and mutation safety boundaries
noveltyJustification: No new security findings. memory_save validates paths and indexability before writes; reconcile attaches and verifies the active shard and fails closed for apply when verification fails.

focus: traceability: public schema, docs, catalog, and operator repair contracts

findingDetails.title: operator docs tell users to run unsupported memory_embedding_reconcile({ dryRun: false })
findingDetails.evidence: The live schema exposes mode: dry-run|apply and rejects unknown parameters, while the install guide and feature catalog still instruct dryRun:false for the reconcile tool.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace stale dryRun:false examples with mode:"apply" and align catalog wording with current buckets/applied response fields.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared tool schema, allowed-parameter validation, handler hint, install guide, and feature catalog.

findingDetails.title: memory_embedding_reconcile accepts activeOnly but never reads it
findingDetails.evidence: activeOnly is accepted in public schemas and TS request types, but runMemoryEmbeddingReconcile only reads mode, resetMissing, requireActiveShard, and repairSuccessCoverage; the handler always attaches the active profile path.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove/deprecate activeOnly or implement explicit semantics for activeOnly:false.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared tool schema, allowed parameters, handler, public request type, and reconcile implementation.

claimAdjudications.claim: Published docs prescribe a rejected or non-applying reconcile call shape.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: Checked system-spec-kit README; it uses mode:"apply", so the drift is not universal. The install guide and feature catalog remain stale and are operator-facing.
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: A caller bypassing schema validation and passing dryRun:false directly to the handler would still default to dry-run rather than apply, so the documented call shape is wrong either way.
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if dryRun is restored as an accepted alias for apply or stale docs are not considered part of the release contract.

results.summary: Scope and read-only review claims were honored; dry-run/apply parity remains partially contradicted by implementation evidence.
No checklist.md exists for this Level 1 slice; no checked completion claims were present to validate.
Feature catalog still advertises dryRun:false for reconcile while live schema uses mode.
Troubleshooting path points operators at the stale dryRun:false call shape.

newFindingsRatioBasis: one net-new P1 plus one net-new P2

focus: maintainability: save sub-handler boundaries and regression coverage gaps
noveltyJustification: No new maintainability findings beyond the active P1/P2 set. The save sub-handler split is internally coherent; the notable gap is regression coverage that covers save/bulk-delete entity-density invalidation but not memory_update.

focus: stabilization: replay active findings and legal-stop gates
newFindingsRatioBasis: stabilization pass found no new findings
